name: Beef Fajitas
recipeIngredient: 2 medium sweet green peppers, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
For marinade, mix 1/4 cup lime juice, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon cooking oil, and 1 clove garlic. Score the meat by making shallow cuts at 1-inch intervals diagonally across the steak on both sides in a diamond pattern. Place the steak in a plastic bag set in a shallow dish. Add the marinade; seal the bag. Turn the steak to coat well. Chill for 6 to 12 hours, turning the steak occasionally. Toss together avocado, salsa, cilantro, 1 tablespoon lime juice, and hot pepper sauce. Cover and chill. Wrap tortillas in foil; set aside.
Tear off a 36x18 inch piece of heavy foil. Fold it in half to make a double thickness that measures 18x18 inches. Place onions and peppers in the center of the foil. Drizzle with 1 tablespoon oil. Bring up two opposite edges of foil and seal. Fold the remaining ends to completely enclose the vegetables, leaving space for steam to build. Place the packet on an uncovered grill directly over medium coals for 10 minutes. Remove the steak from the bag; reserve the marinade. Grill the steak and vegetable packet on an uncovered grill directly over medium coals for 6 minutes. Brush the steak with the marinade; turn the steak. Add the wrapped tortillas to the grill. Grill for 6 to 8 more minutes, or until the beef is desired doneness and vegetables are crisp-tender. To serve, cut the steak into bite-sized strips. Arrange the beef, onions, and peppers on the tortillas. Top with avocado mixture, sour cream, and if desired, tomato. Roll the tortillas around the filling.
